lessons.title: The AI Video Production System
lessons.title_es: El sistema de producción de video con IA
lessons.description: Design a weekly workflow that outputs 30+ short-form videos with a two-person team.
lessons.description_es: Diseña un flujo de trabajo semanal que genere más de 30 videos de formato corto con un equipo de dos personas.
lessons.content:
The 5-Stage Video Factory Model

Publishing 30+ videos per week is not a hustle problem — it's a systems problem. Most content teams are single-threaded: one person writes, shoots, edits, and posts. The Video Factory Model breaks the process into five parallel stages that different tools (and one human) manage simultaneously.

Stage 1 — Ideation (AI-led): Every Monday, prompt Claude or GPT with your niche, your top 5 performing videos of the past month, and current trending audio. Ask for 40 video concepts with hooks. Filter to the top 20 by relevance and uniqueness. This takes 20 minutes.

Stage 2 — Scripting (AI-assisted, human-edited): Use a script template: Hook (0-3s) → Problem agitation (3-8s) → Solution reveal (8-20s) → Proof (20-30s) → CTA (30-35s). Feed each concept into AI with your brand voice guide and generate a first draft. A human edits for authenticity and brand accuracy. Time: 5 minutes per script.

Stage 3 — Production (Batch shooting): Shoot one day per week, 4-6 hours. Prepare all 20 scripts in advance. Use a teleprompter app. Shoot each video 2-3 times for selects. Change tops or backgrounds between batches to create perceived variety. Never shoot individual videos on-demand — batching cuts setup overhead by 80%.

Stage 4 — Editing (AI tool pipeline): Use CapCut, Descript, or Opus Clip for auto-captions. Use AI background removal for talking-head cleanup. Run batch export at all required aspect ratios: 9:16 (TikTok/Reels/Shorts), 1:1 (Feed), 16:9 (YouTube). Editing time per video: 8-12 minutes.

Stage 5 — Distribution (Scheduled, automated): Queue videos in a scheduler (Later, Publer) with pre-written captions and hashtag sets. Post 4-6 videos per day across platforms. Stagger by 2-3 hours.

  • Never publish without captions — captioned videos average 40% higher watch time
  • Build a swipe file of your top hooks and rotate them monthly
  • Create a B-roll library during shoots; it cuts editing time in half
  • Track per-video completion rate — it's the primary ranking signal on all platforms

lessons.title: Hook Engineering: The First 3 Seconds
lessons.title_es: Ingeniería del hook: los primeros 3 segundos
lessons.description: Master the six hook formulas that stop the scroll and maximize watch time on every platform.
lessons.description_es: Domina las seis fórmulas de hook que detienen el scroll y maximizan el tiempo de visualización en cada plataforma.
lessons.content:
The Hook Formula Library

On TikTok, Reels, and YouTube Shorts, 60-70% of viewers drop off in the first 3 seconds. The hook is not a creative nicety — it is the single highest-leverage variable in your video's performance. A great hook on a mediocre video outperforms a mediocre hook on a great video every time.

The Six Hook Formulas:

1. The Bold Claim: State something that sounds impossible or counterintuitive. Example:

No context, no setup — just the number. Works because it creates an information gap the viewer needs to close.

2. The Pattern Interrupt: Start with a visual or sound that's unexpected for your niche. A restaurant showing a spreadsheet, a lawyer doing something physical. The brain stops scrolling when it can't categorize what it's seeing.

3. The Direct Address:

Specificity forces the right viewer to self-identify and keep watching.

4. The Mistake Warning:

Fear of error is a stronger motivator than promise of gain.

5. The Process Reveal:

Step-by-step promises trigger completion bias — viewers feel compelled to watch the full process.

6. The Before/After Setup: Show the end result in the first 2 seconds.

The result creates the question; the video answers it.

Hook Testing Protocol: For each batch of 20 videos, use at least 3 different hook formulas. After 7 days, sort by 3-second retention rate in native analytics. The formula with the highest retention rate becomes the primary template for the next batch.

  • Never open with a greeting (

) — it costs you 30% of your audience immediately
  • Text overlay in the first frame adds a second hook layer for silent viewers
  • Test the same video with two different hooks using A/B post scheduling
  • Build a swipe file: save 50 hooks from competitor videos that made you keep watching

lessons.title: AI Tools Stack: From Script to Published in 90 Minutes
lessons.title_es: Stack de herramientas de IA: del guión a la publicación en 90 minutos
lessons.description: The exact tool chain that cuts video production time by 70% while maintaining quality.
lessons.description_es: La cadena exacta de herramientas que reduce el tiempo de producción de video en un 70% manteniendo la calidad.
lessons.content:
The 90-Minute Production Stack

The right AI tools don't replace judgment — they eliminate repetitive manual work. Here's the exact stack, role of each tool, and how they connect in sequence.

Scripting Layer:

  • Claude/ChatGPT: First-draft scripts using your hook formula + brand voice prompt. Input: topic + hook type. Output: 150-word script with timestamps.
  • Teleprompter app (Teleprompter Premium or Speeko): Load script on mobile, control speed with a Bluetooth clicker. Eliminates memorization time entirely.

Production Layer:

  • DaVinci Resolve or CapCut (Desktop): Primary edit. Cut pauses, remove filler words using waveform view. Target: 3 seconds maximum of dead air in any sequence.
  • Descript: Word-level editing via transcript. Delete filler words (

) by selecting text. Auto-removes corresponding video segments. Saves 20-30 minutes per video.

Enhancement Layer:

  • ElevenLabs or HeyGen: For avatar-based or voiceover-only videos. Clone your voice, generate variations. Useful for repurposing blog content into video without additional shoots.
  • Opus Clip: AI identifies the 3-5 highest-engagement moments in long-form video and exports them as short-form clips with auto-captions. Run each 30-minute podcast or webinar through Opus Clip → generate 5-8 clips in 10 minutes.
  • RunwayML Gen-3: Generate B-roll from text prompts to cover talking-head segments. 30-second B-roll clips cost $0.10-0.30 to generate vs. $50-200 for stock footage licensing.

Distribution Layer:

  • Publer or Later: Queue 30 videos at once. Set posting schedule by platform and time zone. Auto-publish to TikTok, Instagram, YouTube, Facebook simultaneously.

The 90-minute breakdown: Scripting (15 min) → Shoot (20 min) → Descript edit (20 min) → Enhancement (15 min) → Caption + export (10 min) → Scheduling (10 min).

  • Create saved caption templates for each content type to eliminate copy writing time
  • Use consistent background music tracks across batches for brand audio identity
  • Archive every raw clip in organized folders — repurposing old footage costs zero production time
  • Review Opus Clip outputs manually — AI selection is 80% accurate, not 100%

lessons.title: Video SEO and Algorithm Optimization
lessons.title_es: SEO de video y optimización del algoritmo
lessons.description: Reverse-engineer platform algorithms to maximize organic reach and compound views over time.
lessons.description_es: Comprende los algoritmos de las plataformas para maximizar el alcance orgánico y acumular vistas con el tiempo.
lessons.content:
Platform Algorithm Playbook

Every short-form platform shares three core ranking signals, but weights them differently. Understanding the weighting is the difference between videos that plateau at 500 views and videos that hit 500,000.

Universal Signals (all platforms):

  • Watch time / completion rate: The single most important metric. A 60-second video watched to completion is worth 10x more than a 60-second video abandoned at 30 seconds. Structure every video so the payoff lands in the final 10 seconds.
  • Engagement velocity: Likes, comments, and shares in the first 60 minutes. The algorithm tests your video on a small initial audience; strong early engagement triggers wider distribution. Post when your audience is most active (check platform analytics) and respond to every early comment to boost velocity.
  • Rewatch rate: TikTok and YouTube Shorts track whether viewers replay a video. Design videos with multiple layers of value — a fast speaker, dense information, or a visual that rewards re-watching.

Platform-Specific Weights:

TikTok: Prioritizes completion rate above all. Add a CTA at the second-to-last frame (

) to pull viewers through. Use trending audio within 48 hours of it peaking — TikTok provides a distribution boost to trending-audio content.

Instagram Reels: Shares to Stories and DMs are weighted heavily. Design one element in each video that viewers will want to share — a surprising stat, a relatable problem, or a practical checklist.

CTAs directly impact the save rate, which is Instagram's proxy for high-value content.

YouTube Shorts: Click-through rate from the Shorts shelf matters here — thumbnail and title optimization is more important than on TikTok. Use keyword-rich titles, not just clever ones.

Hashtag Strategy: Use 3-5 niche hashtags (under 500K posts), not broad ones. Broad hashtags (10M+ posts) bury your content. Niche hashtags put you at the top of a smaller but highly relevant search. Mix one broad, two medium, and two niche per post.

  • Post at the same time each day — platform algorithms reward consistency with preferential distribution
  • Pin your top three performing videos to your profile — they become permanent lead generators
  • Delete videos that underperform in the first 72 hours; low-engagement content damages account-level distribution scores
  • Cross-post to YouTube Shorts within 24 hours of TikTok/Reels posts for compounded reach
